title: Puliyannoor Mahadeva Temple
tagline: Puliyannoor Mahadeva Temple — spiritual town of Kerala

overview.short: Puliyannoor Mahadeva Temple is a Hindu temple.
overview.description: Puliyannoor Mahadeva Temple is a Hindu temple. It is located in Puliyannoor in Mutholy panchayath and near Pala in Kottayam district in the Indian state of Kerala. it is commonly known as “Cheruthil Valuthu Puliyannoor” (big among the small). The Namboothiri families known as 'Puliyannoor Oorayma Temple Devaswom' administer the temple.

routes.byAir: Fly to nearest airport + road transfer
Fly to nearest airport + road transfer

howToReach.roadNote: Roads are generally good; check seasonal/monsoon conditions before high-altitude or remote stretches.

topPlaces.name: Cherpunkal
topPlaces.description: Cherpunkal is a small town in the Kottayam district of Kerala State in India on the Kottayam-Palai Road, seven kilometers from Pala city. Cherpunkal is famous for Mar Sleeva Forane Catholic Church, believed to have been established by Thomas the Apostle.

topPlaces.name: Mutholy
topPlaces.description: Mutholy is a village in Kottayam district of Kerala State in India on the west side of the City of Pala. Mutholy Gramapanchayath lies on both sides of the Meenachil River.

topPlaces.name: Vallichira
topPlaces.description: Vallichira is a village in Kottayam district in the state of Kerala, India.

topPlaces.name: Kidangoor Subramanya Swami Temple
topPlaces.description: Kidangoor Subramanya Swami Temple is an ancient Hindu temple located in Kidangoor near Ayarkkunnam in Kottayam district in the Indian state of Kerala. It is one of the renowned Subramanya temples in Kerala which is estimated to be at least 1500 years old.

topPlaces.name: Kezhuvamkulam
topPlaces.description: Kezhuvamkulam is a village in the Kottayam District in the Indian state of Kerala.

topPlaces.name: Kadaplamattom
topPlaces.description: Kadaplamattom is a small village near Palai, Kottayam District, Kerala, India. It is a largely agrarian community, with most of the population having agricultural roles within it.

topPlaces.name: Andoor
topPlaces.description: Andoor is a village in the Kottayam district of Kerala, India. The village is located approximately 9 km north-west of Palai on the road towards Kuravilangad.

topPlaces.name: Katappattor
topPlaces.description: Kadappattoor is a small village on the banks of Meenachil river near Pala in Kottayam district of Kerala, 30 km east of Kottayam. The village is the location of the Shiva Kadappattoor Mahadeva Temple (also Kadappatoor Temple or "Katappattur Temple" or "Kadapattur Mahadeva Temple") Thousands of pilgrims visit the Kadappattoor temple every year, on their way to Sabarimala.

items.note: Cherpunkal is a small town in the Kottayam district of Kerala State in India on the Kottayam-Palai Road, seven kilometers from Pala city. Cherpunkal is famous for Mar Sleeva Forane Catholic Church, believed to have been established by Thomas the Apostle.
Mutholy is a village in Kottayam district of Kerala State in India on the west side of the City of Pala. Mutholy Gramapanchayath lies on both sides of the Meenachil River.
Vallichira is a village in Kottayam district in the state of Kerala, India.

items.note: Kidangoor Subramanya Swami Temple is an ancient Hindu temple located in Kidangoor near Ayarkkunnam in Kottayam district in the Indian state of Kerala. It is one of the renowned Subramanya temples in Kerala which is estimated to be at least 1500 years old.
Kezhuvamkulam is a village in the Kottayam District in the Indian state of Kerala.
Kadaplamattom is a small village near Palai, Kottayam District, Kerala, India. It is a largely agrarian community, with most of the population having agricultural roles within it.
Andoor is a village in the Kottayam district of Kerala, India. The village is located approximately 9 km north-west of Palai on the road towards Kuravilangad.

Kadappattoor is a small village on the banks of Meenachil river near Pala in Kottayam district of Kerala, 30 km east of Kottayam. The village is the location of the Shiva Kadappattoor Mahadeva Temple (also Kadappatoor Temple or "Katappattur Temple" or "Kadapattur Mahadeva Temple") Thousands of pilgrims visit the Kadappattoor temple every year, on their way to Sabarimala.

faq.q: What is the best time to visit Puliyannoor Mahadeva Temple?
faq.a: The best time to visit Puliyannoor Mahadeva Temple is Nov – Feb. Summers are 26–36°C and winters are 22–32°C.

faq.q: How do I reach Puliyannoor Mahadeva Temple?
faq.a: The nearest airport is Cochin International Airport, Kochi (56 km) and the nearest railway station is Ernakulam Junction (Kochi) (48 km). Roads are generally good; check seasonal/monsoon conditions before high-altitude or remote stretches.

faq.q: How many days are enough for Puliyannoor Mahadeva Temple?
faq.a: 3 days are usually enough to cover the top attractions of Puliyannoor Mahadeva Temple at a relaxed pace.

faq.q: How much does a hotel cost in Puliyannoor Mahadeva Temple?
faq.a: Stays in Puliyannoor Mahadeva Temple start from ₹900 per night, with options across 4 price categories.

faq.q: What are the top places to visit in Puliyannoor Mahadeva Temple?
faq.a: The top attractions are Cherpunkal, Mutholy, Vallichira, Kidangoor Subramanya Swami Temple, Kezhuvamkulam.
